definition: A family or variety of dimension $1, 2, \ldots, n$.
definition_raw: A family or variety of dimension <math>1, 2, \ldots, n</math>. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.288}}

definition: An archaic name for the symplectic group.
definition_raw: An archaic name for the [[symplectic group]].

definition: The deviation of a curve from circular form. See .
definition_raw: The deviation of a curve from circular form. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p. 356 }}.

definition: A fixed choice of something in projective space, used to construct some other geometry from projective geometry. For example, choosing a plane, called the absolute plane, of projective space can be used to make its complement into a copy of affine space. Choosing a suitable conic or polarity, called the Cayley absolute, absolute conic or absolute polarity, in the absolute plane provides the means to put a metric on affine space so that it becomes a metric space.
definition_raw: A fixed choice of something in projective space, used to construct some other geometry from projective geometry. For example, choosing a plane, called the '''absolute plane''', of projective space can be used to make its complement into a copy of affine space. Choosing a suitable conic or polarity, called the [[Cayley absolute]], '''absolute conic''' or '''absolute polarity''', in the absolute plane provides the means to put a metric on affine space so that it becomes a metric space.

definition: An accidental (or improper) double point of a surface in 4-dimensional projective space is a double point with two distinct tangent planes.
definition_raw: An accidental (or improper) double point of a surface in 4-dimensional projective space is a double point with two distinct tangent planes. {{harv|Baker|1933b|loc=vol 6, p. 157}}

definition: An acnode is an isolated point of a real curve. See .
definition_raw: An [[acnode]] is an isolated point of a real curve. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.23  }}.

definition: If C is a curve, an adjoint of C is a curve such that any point of C of multiplicity r has multiplicity at least r–1 on the adjoint. Sometimes the multiple points of C are required to be ordinary, and if theis condition is not satisfied the term "sub-adjoint" is used.
definition_raw: If ''C'' is a curve, an adjoint of ''C'' is a curve such that any point of ''C'' of multiplicity ''r'' has multiplicity at least ''r''–1 on the adjoint. Sometimes the multiple points of ''C'' are required to be ordinary, and if theis condition is not satisfied the term "sub-adjoint" is used. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.55, 231}}

definition: Affine space is roughly a vector space where one has forgotten which point is the origin.
definition_raw: [[Affine space]] is roughly a vector space where one has forgotten which point is the origin.

definition: An automorphism of affine space.
definition_raw: An automorphism of affine space.

definition: A set.
definition_raw: A set.

definition: An ambient variety is a large variety containing all the points, curves, divisors, and so on that one is interested in.
definition_raw: An [[ambient variety]] is a large variety containing all the points, curves, divisors, and so on that  one is interested in.

definition: Cross-ratio
definition_raw: [[Cross-ratio]]

definition: One of a pair of points constructed from two foci of a curve. See .
definition_raw: One of a pair of points constructed from two foci of a curve. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.119  }}.

definition: An apparent singularity is a singularity of a projection of a variety into a hyperplane. They are so called because they appear to be singularities to an observer at the point being projected from.
definition_raw: An apparent singularity is a singularity of a projection of a variety into a hyperplane. They are so called because they appear to be singularities to an observer at the point being projected from. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.55, 231}}

definition: Orthogonal under the polar pairing between the symmetric algebra of a vector space and its dual.
definition_raw: Orthogonal under the polar pairing between the symmetric algebra of a vector space and its dual.

definition: The arithmetic genus of a variety is a variation of the Euler characteristic of the trivial line bundle; see Hodge number.
definition_raw: The [[arithmetic genus]] of a variety is a variation of the Euler characteristic of the trivial line bundle; see [[Hodge number]].

definition: One of the 288 sets of 7 of the 28 bitangents of a quartic curve corresponding to the 7 odd theta characteristics of a normal set.
definition_raw: One of the 288 sets of 7 of the 28 bitangents of a quartic curve corresponding to the 7 odd theta characteristics of a normal set.

definition: An associated curve is the image of a projective curve in a Grassmannian, given by taking the tangent lines, or osculating planes, and so on.
definition_raw: An associated curve is the image of a projective curve in a Grassmannian, given by taking the tangent lines, or osculating planes, and so on.

definition: A special line or linear subspace associated with some family of geometric objects. For example, a special linear complex in 4-dimensional space consists of all lines meeting a given plane, that is called the axial plane of the complex. Similar to directrix.
definition_raw: A special line or linear subspace associated with some family of geometric objects. For example, a special linear complex in 4-dimensional space consists of all lines meeting a given plane, that is called the axial plane of the complex. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.274}} Similar to directrix.

definition: A special line or linear subspace associated with some family of geometric objects. For example, a special linear complex in 4-dimensional space consists of all lines meeting a given plane, that is called the axial plane of the complex. Similar to directrix.

definition: Unpaired. Opposite of syzygetic, meaning paired. Example: azygetic triad, azygetic tetrad, azygetic set.
definition_raw: Unpaired. Opposite of syzygetic, meaning paired. Example: azygetic triad, azygetic tetrad, azygetic set.

definition: A base point is a point common to all members of a family.
definition_raw: A '''base point''' is a point common to all members of a family.

definition: Having nodes at the two circular points at infinity, as in bicircular curve. See .
definition_raw: Having nodes at the two circular points at infinity, as in [[bicircular curve]]. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.231  }}.

definition: A bicorn is a curve with two cusps.
definition_raw: A [[bicorn]] is a curve with two cusps.

definition_raw: A bielliptic curve is a branched double cover of an elliptic curve.

definition_raw: Same as fleflecnode. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.210}}.

definition_raw: A [[biregular map]] is a regular map with regular inverse

definition: A plane conic passing through the circular points at infinity. definition: A (mixed) concomitant is an invariant homogeneous polynomial in the coefficients of a form, a covariant variable, and a contravariant variable. In other words it is a (tri)homogeneous polynomial on SV⊕V⊕V* for some vector space V, where SV is some symmetric power of V and V* its dual, that is invariant under the special linear group of V. In practice V often has dimension 2. The degree, class, and order of a concomitant are its degrees in the three types of variable. Concomitants are generalizations of covariants, contravariants, and invariants.

In other words it is a (tri)homogeneous polynomial on ''SV''⊕''V''⊕''V''* for some vector space ''V'', where ''SV'' is some symmetric power of ''V'' and ''V''* its dual, that is invariant under the special linear group of ''V''. definition: A bihomogeneous polynomial in dual variables of x, y, ... and the coefficients of some homogeneous form in x, y,... that is invariant under some group of linear transformations. In other words it is a bihomogeneous polynomial on SV⊕V for some vector space V, where SV is some symmetric power of V and V* its dual, that is invariant under the special linear group of V. In practice V often has dimension at least 3, because when it has dimension 2 these are more or less the same as covariants. The degree and class of a contravariant are its degrees in the two types of variable. Contravariants generalize invariants and are special cases of concomitants, and are in some sense dual to covariants.

definition_raw: An [[inversive geometry|inversion]] is a transformation of order 2 exchanging the inside and outside of a circle. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.103  }}.

definition: An involute is a curve obtained by unrolling a string around a curve. See .
definition_raw: An [[involute]] is a curve obtained by unrolling a string around a curve. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p. 278 }}.

definition: A transformation whose square is the identity. Cremona transformations that are involutions include Bertini involutions, Geiser involutions, and De Jonquières involutions.
definition_raw: A transformation whose square is the identity.  [[Cremona transformation]]s that are involutions include [[Bertini involution]]s, [[Geiser involution]]s, and [[De Jonquières involution]]s.

definition: The irregularity of a surface is the dimension of the space of holomorphic 1-forms on a non-singular projective surface; see Hodge number.
definition_raw: The [[irregularity of a surface]] is the dimension of the space of holomorphic 1-forms on a non-singular projective surface; see [[Hodge number]].

definition: Given a Cremoma transformation T, the isologue of a point p is the set of points x such that p, x, T(x) are collinear. The point p is called the center of the isologue.
definition_raw: Given a Cremoma transformation ''T'', the isologue of a point ''p'' is the set of points ''x'' such that ''p'', ''x'', ''T''(''x'') are collinear. The point ''p'' is called the center of the isologue.

definition: The multiplicity of a point on a hypersurface is the degree of the first non-vanishing coefficient of the Taylor series at the point. definition: A polygonal (or k-gonal) curve is a curve together with a map (of degree k) to the projective line. The degree of the map is called the gonality of the curve. When the degree is 1, 2, or 3 the curve is called rational, hyperelliptic, or trigonal.

definition: A porism is a corollary, especially in geometry, as in Poncelet's porism. The precise meaning seems to be controversial.
definition_raw: A [[porism]] is a corollary, especially in geometry, as in [[Poncelet's porism]]. The precise meaning seems to be controversial.

definition: Having either no solutions or infinitely many . For example, Poncelet's porism and Steiner's porism imply that if there is one way to arrange lines or circles then there are infinitely many ways.
definition_raw: Having either no solutions or infinitely many {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.186}}. For example, [[Poncelet's porism]] and [[Steiner's porism]] imply that if there is one way to arrange lines or circles then there are infinitely many ways.

definition: A postulated object (point, line, and so on) is an object in some larger space. For example, a point at infinity of projective space is a postulated point of affine space.
definition_raw: A postulated object (point, line, and so on) is an object in some larger space. For example, a point at infinity of projective space is a postulated point of affine space. {{harv|Baker|1922a|loc=vol 1, {{pn|date=June 2020}}}}

definition: The postulation of a variety for some family is the number of independent conditions needed to force an elements of the family to contain the variety.
definition_raw: The postulation of a variety for some family is the number of independent conditions needed to force an elements of the family to contain the variety. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.440}}

definition: Laguerre defined the power of a point with respect to an algebraic curve of degree n to be the product of the distances from the point to the intersections with a circle through it, divided by the nth power of the diameter. He showed that this is independent of the choice of circle through the point.

definition_raw: A family of algebraic sets in projective space; for example, a line system is a family of lines.

definition: Paired. Opposite of azygetic, meaning unpaired. Example: syzygetic triad, syzygetic tetrad, syzygetic set, syzygetic pencil.
definition_raw: Paired. Opposite of azygetic, meaning unpaired. Example: syzygetic triad, syzygetic tetrad, syzygetic set, [[syzygetic pencil]].

definition: A point is in syzygy with some other points if it is in the linear subspace generated by them. A syzygy is a linear relation between points in an affine space.
definition_raw: A point is in syzygy with some other points if it is in the linear subspace generated by them. {{harv|Baker|1922a|loc=vol 1, p. 33}} A syzygy is a linear relation between points in an affine space.

definition: A tacnode is a point of a curve where two branches meet in the same direction.
definition_raw: A [[tacnode]] is a point of a curve where two branches meet in the same direction. {{harv|Cayley|1852}}

definition: A tangent cone is a cone defined by the non-zero terms of smallest degree in the Taylor series at a point of a hypersurface.
definition_raw: A [[tangent cone]] is a cone defined by the non-zero terms of smallest degree in the Taylor series at a point of a hypersurface.

definition: The tangential equation of a plane curve is an equation giving the condition for a line to be tangent to the curve. In other words it is the equation of the dual curve. It is not the equation of a tangent to a curve.
definition_raw: The tangential equation of a plane curve is an equation giving the condition for a line to be tangent to the curve. In other words it is the equation of the dual curve. It is not the equation of a tangent to a curve.

definition_raw: A set of 4 points

definition: A geometric configuration consisting of 4 points and the 6 lines joining pairs. This is similar to the lines and infinite edges of a polyhedral tetrahedron, but in algebraic geometry one sometimes does not include the faces of the tetrahedron.
definition_raw: A geometric configuration consisting of 4 points and the 6 lines joining pairs. This is similar to the lines and infinite edges of a polyhedral [[tetrahedron]], but in algebraic geometry one sometimes does not include the faces of the tetrahedron.

definition_raw: A trigonal curve is one with a degree three map to the projective line. See hyperelliptic.

definition_raw: Having three connected components. {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.165  }}

definition: A line meeting a variety in 3 points. See trisecant identity.
definition_raw: A line meeting a variety in 3 points. See [[trisecant identity]].

definition: Meeting something in 3 tangent points, such as a tritangent conic to a cubic curve or a tritangent plane of a cubic surface.
definition_raw: Meeting something in 3 tangent points, such as a tritangent conic to a cubic curve or a tritangent plane of a cubic surface.

definition: A trope is a singular (meaning special) tangent space. The word is mostly used for a tangent space of a Kummer surface touching it along a conic.

definition: A twisted cubic is a degree 3 embedding of the projective line in projective 3-space
definition_raw: A [[twisted cubic]] is a degree 3 embedding of the projective line in projective 3-space

definition: A set of 5 partitions of a 6-element set into three pairs, such that no two elements of the total have a pair in common. For example, {(12)(36)(45), (13)(24)(56), (14)(26)(35), (15)(23)(46), (16)(25)(34)}
definition_raw: A set of 5 partitions of a 6-element set into three pairs, such that no two elements of the total have a pair in common. For example,  {(12)(36)(45), (13)(24)(56), (14)(26)(35), (15)(23)(46), (16)(25)(34)} {{harv|Dolgachev|2012}}

definition: The type of a projective surface is the number of tangent planes meeting a generic linear subspace of codimension 4.
definition_raw: The type of a projective surface is the number of tangent planes meeting a generic linear subspace of codimension 4. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.193}}

definition: A point of undulation of a curve is where the tangent meets the curve to fourth order; also called a hyperflex. See inflection point.
definition_raw: A point of undulation of a curve is where the tangent meets the curve to fourth order; also called a hyperflex. See inflection point. {{harv|Salmon|1879|loc=p.35, 211  }}

definition: Having only one branch at a point. For example, a cusp of a plane curve is unibranch, while a node is not.
definition_raw: Having only one branch at a point. For example, a cusp of a plane curve is unibranch, while a node is not.

definition: A unicursal curve is one that is rational, in other words birational to the projective line. See .
definition_raw: A unicursal curve is one that is [[rational variety|rational]], in other words birational to the projective line. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p. 29 }}.

definition: Connected. See
definition_raw: [[Connected (topology)|Connected]]. See {{harvtxt|Salmon|1879|loc=p.165  }}

definition: A correspondence is called unirational if it is generically injective, in other words a rational map.
definition_raw: A correspondence is called unirational if it is generically injective, in other words a rational map. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.20}}

definition: A point in the intersection of the diagonal and a correspondence from a set to itself.
definition_raw: A point in the intersection of the diagonal and a correspondence from a set to itself.

definition: A double point of a surface whose tangent cone consists of one double plane. See binode.
definition_raw: A double point of a surface whose tangent cone consists of one double plane. See binode.

definition: The valence or valency of a correspondence T on a curve is a number k such that the divisors T(P)+kP are all linearly equivalent. A correspondence need not have a valency.
definition_raw: The valence or valency of a correspondence ''T'' on a curve is a number ''k'' such that the divisors  ''T''(''P'')+''kP'' are all linearly equivalent. A correspondence need not have a valency. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.368}}

definition: The valence or valency of a correspondence T on a curve is a number k such that the divisors T(P)+kP are all linearly equivalent. A correspondence need not have a valency.
definition_raw: The valence or valency of a correspondence ''T'' on a curve is a number ''k'' such that the divisors  ''T''(''P'')+''kP'' are all linearly equivalent. A correspondence need not have a valency. {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.368}}

definition: An embedding of the projective plane in 5-dimensional projective space.
definition_raw: {{main|Veronese surface}} An embedding of the projective plane in 5-dimensional projective space.

definition: An estimate for something that is often but not always correct, such as virtual genus, virtual dimension, and so on. If some number is given by the dimension of a space of sections of some sheaf, the corresponding virtual number is sometimes given by the corresponding Euler characteristic, and equal to the dimension when all higher cohomology groups vanish. See superabundance.
definition_raw: An estimate for something that is often but not always correct, such as virtual genus, virtual dimension, and so on. If some number is given by the dimension of a space of sections of some sheaf, the corresponding virtual number is sometimes given by the corresponding Euler characteristic, and equal to the dimension when all higher cohomology groups vanish. See superabundance.

definition: A 3-dimensional linear system. See "net" and "pencil".
definition_raw: A 3-dimensional linear system. See "net" and "pencil". {{harv|Semple|Roth|1949|loc=p.160}}

definition: A quartic surface in projective space given by the locus of the vertex of a cone passing through 6 points in general position.
definition_raw: {{main|Weddle surface}} A quartic surface in projective space given by the locus of the vertex of a cone passing through 6 points in general position.

definition: A point on a curve where the dimension of the space of rational functions whose only singularity is a pole of some order at the point is higher than normal.
definition_raw: {{Main|Weierstrass point}} A point on a curve where the dimension of the space of rational functions whose only singularity is a pole of some order at the point is higher than normal.

definition: A degree 4 genus 6 plane curve with nodes at the 6 points of a complete quadrangle.
definition_raw: {{main|Wirtinger sextic}} A degree 4 genus 6 plane curve with nodes at the 6 points of a [[complete quadrangle]].

definition: The Zeuthen–Segre invariant is 4 less than the Euler characteristic of a non-singular projective surface.
definition_raw: The [[Zeuthen–Segre invariant]] is 4 less than the Euler characteristic of a non-singular projective surface.

definition: k(x,y,...) is a field extension of k generated by x,y,...
definition_raw: ''k''(''x'',''y'',...) is a [[field extension]] of  ''k'' generated by ''x'',''y'',...

definition: R[x,y,...] is a polynomial ring over R.
definition_raw: ''R''[''x'',''y'',...] is a [[polynomial ring]] over ''R''.

definition: Rx,y,... is a formal power series ring over R.
definition_raw: ''R''<nowiki>[[</nowiki>''x'',''y'',...<nowiki>]]</nowiki> is a [[formal power series ring]] over ''R''.

definition: R{x,y,...} is a ring of formal power series over R satisfying some convergence condition.
definition_raw: ''R''{''x'',''y'',...} is a ring of formal power series over ''R'' satisfying some convergence condition.

definition: Â is the completion of A.
definition_raw: ''Â'' is the [[completion (ring theory)|completion]] of ''A''.

definition: The absolute integral closure is the integral closure of an integral domain in an algebraic closure of the field of fractions of the domain.
definition_raw: The [[absolute integral closure]] is the integral closure of an integral domain in an algebraic closure of the field of fractions of the domain.

definition: The word "absolutely" usually means "not relatively"; i.e., independent of the base field in some sense. It is often synonymous with "geometrically".
definition_raw: The word "absolutely" usually means "not relatively"; i.e., independent of the base field in some sense. It is often synonymous with "geometrically".

definition: Acceptable rings are generalizations of excellent rings, with the conditions about regular rings in the definition replaced by conditions about Gorenstein rings.
definition_raw: [[Acceptable ring]]s are generalizations of [[excellent rings]], with the conditions about regular rings in the definition replaced by conditions about Gorenstein rings.

definition: The I-adic topology on a ring has a base of neighborhoods of 0 given by powers of the ideal I.
definition_raw: The ''I''-adic topology on a ring has a base of neighborhoods of 0 given by powers of the ideal ''I''.

definition: An affine ring R over another ring S (often a field) is a ring (or sometimes an integral domain) that is finitely generated over S.
definition_raw: An affine ring ''R'' over another ring ''S'' (often a field) is a ring (or sometimes an integral domain) that is finitely generated over ''S''.

definition: A local ring that is a localization of a finitely-generated domain over a field.
definition_raw: A local ring that is a localization of a finitely-generated domain over a field.

definition: An element x of a ring is called almost integral over a subring if there is a regular element a of the subring so that axn is in the subring for all positive integers n.
definition_raw: An element ''x'' of a ring is called almost integral over a subring if there is a regular element ''a'' of the subring so that ''ax''<sup>''n''</sup> is in the subring for all positive integers ''n''.

definition: The altitude of a ring is an archaic name for its dimension.
definition_raw: The '''altitude''' of a ring is an archaic name for its dimension.

definition: The analytic spread of an ideal of a local ring is the Krull dimension of the fiber at the special point of the local ring of the Rees algebra of the ideal.
definition_raw: The analytic spread of an ideal of a local ring is the Krull dimension of the fiber at the special point of the local ring of the Rees algebra of the ideal.

definition: This often refers to properties of the completion of a local ring; cf. #formally
definition_raw: This often refers to properties of the completion of a local ring; cf. [[#formally]]

definition: The annihilator of a subset of a module is the ideal of elements whose product with any element of the subset is 0.
definition_raw: The [[Annihilator (ring theory)|annihilator]] of a subset of a module is the ideal of elements whose product with any element of the subset is 0.

definition: Emil Artin
definition_raw: [[Emil Artin]]

definition: Emil Artin
definition_raw: [[Emil Artin]]

definition: Acronym for algebra with straightening law.
definition_raw: Acronym for [[algebra with straightening law]].

definition: An associated prime of a module M over a ring R is a prime ideal p such that M has a submodule isomorphic to R/p.
definition_raw: An [[associated prime]] of a module ''M'' over a ring ''R'' is a prime ideal ''p'' such that ''M'' has a submodule isomorphic to ''R''/''p''.

definition: If M is a module over a local ring R with residue field k, then the ith Bass number of M is the k-dimension of Ext(k,M).
definition_raw: If ''M'' is a module over a local ring ''R'' with residue field ''k'', then the ''i''th [[Bass number]] of ''M'' is the ''k''-dimension of Ext{{su|p=''i''|b=''R''}}(''k'',''M'').

definition: A Bézout domain is an integral domain in which the sum of two principal ideals is a principal ideal.
definition_raw: A  [[Bézout domain]] is an integral domain in which the sum of two principal ideals is a principal ideal.

definition: The word "big" when applied to a module emphasizes that the module is not necessarily finitely generated. In particular a big Cohen–Macaulay module is a module that has a system of parameters for which it is regular.
definition_raw: The word "big" when applied to a module emphasizes that the module is not necessarily finitely generated. In particular a big Cohen–Macaulay module is a module that has a system of parameters for which it is regular.

definition: A Bourbaki ideal of a torsion-free module M is an ideal isomorphic (as a module) to a torsion-free quotient of M by a free submodule.
definition_raw: A Bourbaki ideal of a torsion-free module ''M'' is an ideal isomorphic (as a module) to a torsion-free quotient of ''M'' by a free submodule.

definition: A Buchsbaum ring is a Noetherian local ring such that every system of parameters is a weak sequence.
definition_raw: A [[Buchsbaum ring]] is a Noetherian local ring such that every system of parameters is a weak sequence.

definition: "Canonical module" is an alternative term for a dualizing module.
definition_raw: "Canonical module" is an alternative term for a [[dualizing module]].

definition: A ring is called catenary if all maximal chains between two prime ideals have the same length.
definition_raw: A ring is called [[Catenary (ring theory)|catenary]] if all maximal chains between two prime ideals have the same length.

definition: The center of a valuation (or place) is the ideal of elements of positive order.
definition_raw: The center of a valuation (or place) is the ideal of elements of positive order.

definition: A strictly increasing or decreasing sequence of prime ideals.
definition_raw: A strictly increasing or decreasing sequence of prime ideals.

definition: The characteristic of a ring is a non-negative integer generating the Z-ideal of multiples of 1 that are zero.
definition_raw: The [[characteristic of a ring]] is a non-negative integer generating the '''Z'''-ideal of multiples of 1 that are zero.

definition: A finitely generated module M over a Noetherian ring R is called clean if it has a finite filtration all of whose quotients are of the form R/p for p an associated prime of M. A stronger variation of this definition says that the primes p must be minimal primes of the support of M.
definition_raw: A finitely generated module ''M'' over a Noetherian ring ''R'' is called clean if it has a finite filtration all of whose quotients are of the form ''R''/''p'' for ''p'' an associated prime of ''M''. A stronger variation of this definition says that the primes ''p'' must be minimal primes of the support of ''M''.

definition: Abbreviation for Cohen–Macaulay.
definition_raw: Abbreviation for [[Cohen–Macaulay ring|Cohen–Macaulay]].

definition: The CoCoA computer algebra system for computations in commutative algebra
definition_raw: The [[CoCoA]] computer algebra system for computations in commutative algebra

definition: The codepth of a finitely generated module over a Noetherian local ring is its dimension minus its depth.
definition_raw: The codepth of a finitely generated module over a Noetherian local ring is its dimension minus its depth.

definition: The codimension of a prime ideal is another name for its #height.
definition_raw: The codimension of a prime ideal is another name for its [[#height]].

definition: A complete Noetherian local ring
definition_raw: A complete Noetherian local ring

definition: Irvin Cohen
definition_raw: [[Irvin Cohen]]

definition: A local ring is called Cohen–Macaulay if it is Noetherian and the Krull dimension is equal to the depth. A ring is called Cohen–Macaulay if it is Noetherian and all localizations at maximal ideals are Cohen–Macaulay.
definition_raw:
A local ring is called [[Cohen–Macaulay ring|Cohen–Macaulay]] if it is Noetherian and the Krull dimension is equal to the depth.
A ring is called Cohen–Macaulay if it is Noetherian and all localizations at maximal ideals are Cohen–Macaulay.

definition: A module is called coherent if it is finitely generated and every homomorphism to it from a finitely generated module has a finitely generated kernel.
definition_raw: A module is called [[coherent module|coherent]] if it is finitely generated and every homomorphism to it from a finitely generated module has a finitely generated kernel.

definition: A local complete intersection ring is a Noetherian local ring whose completion is the quotient of a regular local ring by an ideal generated by a regular sequence.
definition_raw: A [[local complete intersection ring]] is a Noetherian local ring whose completion is the quotient of a regular local ring by an ideal generated by a regular sequence.

definition: A domain R is called completely integrally closed if, whenever all positive powers of some element x of the quotient field are contained in a finitely generated R module, x is in R.
definition_raw: A domain ''R'' is called [[completely integrally closed]] if, whenever all positive powers of some element ''x'' of the quotient field are contained in a finitely generated ''R'' module, ''x'' is in ''R''.

definition: The completion of a module or ring M at an ideal I is the inverse limit of the modules M/InM.
definition_raw: The [[completion of a module]] or ring ''M'' at an ideal ''I'' is the inverse limit of the modules ''M''/''I''<sup>''n''</sup>''M''.

definition: Not prime
definition_raw: Not prime

definition: The conductor of an integral domain R is the annihilator of the R-module T/R, where T is the integral closure of R in its quotient field.
definition_raw: The [[conductor (ring theory)|conductor]] of an integral domain ''R'' is the annihilator of the ''R''-module ''T''/''R'', where ''T'' is the integral closure of ''R'' in its quotient field.

definition: A congruence ideal of a surjective homomorphism f:B→C of commutative rings is the image under f of the annihilator of the kernel of f.
definition_raw: A [[congruence ideal]] of a surjective homomorphism ''f'':''B''→''C'' of commutative rings is the image under ''f'' of the annihilator of the kernel of ''f''.

definition: A graded algebra over a field k is connected if its zeroth degree piece is k.
definition_raw: A graded algebra over a field ''k'' is connected if its zeroth degree piece is ''k''.

definition: The conormal module of a quotient of a ring by an ideal I is the module I/I2.
definition_raw: The conormal module of a quotient of a ring by an ideal ''I'' is the module ''I''/''I''<sup>2</sup>.

definition: For a Noetherian ring, a constructible subset of the spectrum is one that is a finite union of locally closed sets. For rings that are not Noetherian the definition of a constructible subset is more complicated.
definition_raw: For a Noetherian ring, a [[Constructible set (topology)|constructible subset]] of the spectrum is one that is a finite union of locally closed sets. For rings that are not Noetherian the definition of a constructible subset is more complicated.

definition: The content of a polynomial is a greatest common divisor of its coefficients.
definition_raw: The content of a polynomial is a greatest common divisor of its coefficients.

definition: The contraction of an ideal is the ideal given by the inverse image of some ideal under a homomorphism of rings.
definition_raw: The [[contraction of an ideal]] is the ideal given by the inverse image of some ideal under a homomorphism of rings.

definition: |A coprimary module is a module with exactly one associated prime.
definition_raw: |A [[coprimary module]] is a module with exactly one associated prime.

definition: Two ideals are called coprime if their sum is the whole ring.
definition_raw: Two ideals are called coprime if their sum is the whole ring.

definition: The cotangent space of a local ring with maximal ideal m is the vector space m/m2 over the residue field.
definition_raw: The [[cotangent space]] of a local ring with maximal ideal ''m'' is the vector space ''m''/''m''<sup>2</sup> over the residue field.

definition: A Cox ring is a sort of universal homogeneous coordinate ring for a projective variety.
definition_raw: A [[Cox ring]] is a sort of universal homogeneous coordinate ring for a projective variety.

definition: A module is called decomposable if it can be written as a direct sum of two non-zero submodules.
definition_raw: A module is called [[indecomposable module|decomposable]] if it can be written as a direct sum of two non-zero submodules.

definition: A decomposition group is a group of automorphisms of a ring whose elements fix a given prime ideal.
definition_raw: A [[decomposition group]] is a group of automorphisms of a ring whose elements fix a given prime ideal.

definition: A Dedekind domain is a Noetherian integrally closed domain of dimension at most 1.
definition_raw: A [[Dedekind domain]] is a Noetherian integrally closed domain of dimension at most 1.

definition: The ramification defect or ramification deficiency d of a valuation of a field K is given by [L:K]=defg where e is the ramification index, f is the inertia degree, and g is the number of extensions of the valuation to a larger field L. The number d is a power pδ of the characteristic p, and sometimes δ rather than d is called the ramification deficiency.
definition_raw: The '''ramification defect''' or '''ramification deficiency''' ''d'' of a valuation of a field ''K'' is given by [''L'':''K'']=''defg'' where ''e'' is the ramification index, ''f'' is the inertia degree, and ''g'' is the number of extensions of the valuation to a larger field ''L''. The number ''d'' is a power ''p''<sup>δ</sup> of the characteristic ''p'', and sometimes δ rather than ''d'' is called the ramification deficiency.

definition: The ramification defect or ramification deficiency d of a valuation of a field K is given by [L:K]=defg where e is the ramification index, f is the inertia degree, and g is the number of extensions of the valuation to a larger field L. The number d is a power pδ of the characteristic p, and sometimes δ rather than d is called the ramification deficiency.
definition_raw: The '''ramification defect''' or '''ramification deficiency''' ''d'' of a valuation of a field ''K'' is given by [''L'':''K'']=''defg'' where ''e'' is the ramification index, ''f'' is the inertia degree, and ''g'' is the number of extensions of the valuation to a larger field ''L''. The number ''d'' is a power ''p''<sup>δ</sup> of the characteristic ''p'', and sometimes δ rather than ''d'' is called the ramification deficiency.

definition: The I-depth (also called grade) of a module M over a ring R, where I is an ideal, is the smallest integer n such that Ext(R/I,M) is nonzero. When I is the maximal ideal of a local ring this is just called the depth of M, and if in addition M is the local ring R this is called the depth of the ring R.
definition_raw: The [[depth (ring theory)|I-depth]] (also called '''grade''') of a module ''M'' over a ring ''R'', where ''I'' is an ideal, is the smallest integer ''n'' such that Ext{{su|p=''n''|b=''R''}}(''R''/''I'',''M'') is nonzero. When ''I'' is the maximal ideal of a local ring this is just called the depth of ''M'', and if in addition ''M'' is the local ring ''R'' this is called the depth of the ring ''R''.

definition: An additive homomorphism d from a ring to a module that satisfies Leibniz's rule d(ab)=ad(b)+bd(a).
definition_raw: An additive homomorphism ''d'' from a ring to a module that satisfies Leibniz's rule ''d''(''ab'')=''ad''(''b'')+''bd''(''a'').

definition: The derived normal ring of an integral domain is its integral closure in its quotient field.
definition_raw: The '''derived normal ring''' of an integral domain is its integral closure in its quotient field.

definition: The determinant module of a module is the top exterior power of the module.
definition_raw: The [[determinant module]] of a module is the top exterior power of the module.

definition: This often refers to properties of an ideal generated by determinants of minors of a matrix. For example, a determinantal ring is generated by the entries of a matrix, with relations given by the determinants of the minors of some fixed size.
definition_raw: This often refers to properties of an ideal generated by determinants of minors of a matrix. For example, a [[determinantal ring]] is generated by the entries of a matrix, with relations given by the determinants of the minors of some fixed size.

definition: A deviation of a local ring is an invariant that measures how far the ring is from being regular.
definition_raw: A [[deviation of a local ring]] is an invariant that measures how far the ring is from being regular.

definition: The Krull dimension of a ring, often just called the dimension, is the maximal length of a chain of prime ideals, and the Krull dimension of a module is the maximal length of a chain of prime ideals containing its annihilator.
definition_raw: The [[Krull dimension]] of a ring, often just called the dimension, is the maximal length of a chain of prime ideals, and the Krull dimension of a module  is the maximal length of a chain of prime ideals containing its annihilator.

definition: A discrete valuation ring is an integrally closed Noetherian local ring of dimension 1.
definition_raw: A [[discrete valuation ring]] is an integrally closed Noetherian local ring of dimension 1.

definition: A divisible module is a module such that multiplication by any regular element of the ring is surjective.
definition_raw: A [[divisible module]] is a module such that multiplication by any regular element of the ring is surjective.

definition: A divisor of an integral domain is an equivalence class of non-zero fractional ideals, where two such ideals are called equivalent if they are contained in the same principal fractional ideals.
definition_raw: A divisor of an integral domain is an equivalence class of non-zero fractional ideals, where two such ideals are called equivalent if they are contained in the same principal fractional ideals.

definition: A divisorial ideal of an integral domain is a non-zero fractional ideal that is an intersection of principal fractional ideals.
definition_raw: A [[divisorial ideal]] of an integral domain is a non-zero fractional ideal that is an intersection of principal fractional ideals.

definition: A domain or integral domain is a ring with no zero-divisors and where 1≠0.
definition_raw: A domain or [[integral domain]] is a ring with no zero-divisors and where 1≠0.

definition: A local ring B is said to dominate a local ring A if it contains A and the maximal ideal of B contains the maximal ideal of A.
definition_raw: A local ring ''B'' is said to dominate a local ring ''A'' if it contains ''A'' and the maximal ideal of ''B'' contains the maximal ideal of ''A''.

definition: Grothendieck local duality is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.
definition_raw: [[Grothendieck local duality]] is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.

definition: Grothendieck local duality is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.
definition_raw: [[Grothendieck local duality]] is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.

definition: Grothendieck local duality is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.
definition_raw: [[Grothendieck local duality]] is a duality for cohomology of modules over a local ring.

definition: Abbreviation for discrete valuation ring.
definition_raw: Abbreviation for [[discrete valuation ring]].

definition: The Eakin–Nagata theorem states: given a finite ring extension $A \subset B$, $A$ is a Noetherian ring if and only if $B$ is a Noetherian ring.
definition_raw: The [[Eakin–Nagata theorem]] states: given a finite ring extension <math>A \subset B</math>, <math>A</math> is a Noetherian ring if and only if <math>B</math> is a Noetherian ring.

definition: Named after Gotthold Eisenstein
definition_raw: Named after [[Gotthold Eisenstein]]

definition: An embedded prime of a module is a non-minimal associated prime.
definition_raw: An embedded prime of a module is a non-minimal associated prime.

definition: See dimension.
definition_raw: See [[#dimension|dimension]].

definition: An injective envelope (or hull) of a module is a minimal injective module containing it.
definition_raw: An [[injective envelope]] (or hull) of a module is a minimal injective module containing it.

definition: A local ring is called equicharacteristic if it has the same characteristic as its residue field.
definition_raw: A local ring is called equicharacteristic if it has the same characteristic as its residue field.

definition: A submodule M of N is called an essential submodule if it intersects every nonzero submodule of N.
definition_raw: A submodule ''M'' of ''N'' is called an [[essential submodule]] if it intersects every nonzero submodule of ''N''.

definition: An algebra is said to be essentially of finite type over another algebra if it is a localization of a finitely generated algebra.
definition_raw: An algebra is said to be essentially of finite type over another algebra if it is a localization of a finitely generated algebra.

definition: A morphism of rings is called étale if it is formally etale and locally finitely presented.
definition_raw: A morphism of rings is called  [[étale morphism|étale]] if it is formally etale and locally finitely presented.

definition: A Euclidean domain is an integral domain with a form of Euclid's algorithm.
definition_raw: A [[Euclidean domain]] is an integral domain with a form of [[Euclid's algorithm]].

definition: A zero divisor $x$ is said to be an exact zero divisor if its annihilator, $\operatorname{Ann}_R(x)=\{ r\in R\mid rx=0 \}$, is a principal ideal $yR$ whose annihilator is $ xR$: $\operatorname{Ann}_R(x)=\{ r\in R\mid rx=0 \}= yR$ and $\operatorname{Ann}_R(y)=\{ r\in R\mid ry=0 \}= xR$.
definition_raw:
A zero divisor <math>x</math> is said to be an '''exact zero divisor''' if its annihilator, <math>\operatorname{Ann}_R(x)=\{ r\in R\mid rx=0 \}</math>, is a principal ideal <math>yR</math> whose annihilator is <math> xR</math>:
<math>\operatorname{Ann}_R(x)=\{ r\in R\mid rx=0 \}= yR</math> and
<math>\operatorname{Ann}_R(y)=\{ r\in R\mid ry=0 \}= xR</math>.

definition: An excellent ring is a universally catenary Grothendieck ring such that for every finitely generated algebra the singular points of the spectrum form a closed subset.
definition_raw: An [[excellent ring]] is a universally catenary Grothendieck ring  such that for every finitely generated algebra the singular points of the spectrum form a closed subset.

definition: The Ext functors, the derived functors of the Hom functor.
definition_raw: The [[Ext functor]]s, the derived functors of the Hom functor.

definition: An extension of an ideal is the ideal generated by the image under a homomorphism of rings.
definition_raw: An [[extension of an ideal]] is the ideal generated by the image under a homomorphism of rings.

definition: An alternative name for a Stanley–Reisner ring.
definition_raw: An alternative name for a [[Stanley–Reisner ring]].

definition: Factorial ring is an alternative name for a unique factorization domain.
definition_raw: [[Factorial ring]] is an alternative name for a unique factorization domain.

definition: A faithful module is a module whose annihilator is 0.
definition_raw: A [[faithful module]] is a module whose annihilator is 0.

definition: A faithfully flat module over a ring R is a flat module whose tensor product with any non-zero module is non-zero.
definition_raw: A [[faithfully flat module]] over a ring ''R'' is a flat module whose tensor product with any non-zero module is non-zero.

definition: A commutative ring such that every nonzero element has an inverse
definition_raw: A commutative ring such that every nonzero element has an inverse

definition: A finite module (or algebra) over a ring usually means one that is finitely generated as a module. It may also mean one with a finite number of elements, especially in the term finite field.
definition_raw: A finite module (or algebra) over a ring usually means one that is finitely generated as a module. It may also mean one with a finite number of elements, especially in the term [[finite field]].

definition: An algebra over a ring is said to be of finite type if it is finitely generated as an algebra.
definition_raw: An algebra over a ring is said to be of finite type if it is finitely generated as an algebra.

definition: A module over a ring is called finitely generated if every element is a linear combination of a fixed finite number of elements. If the module happens to be an algebra this is much stronger than saying it is finitely generated as an algebra.
definition_raw: A module over a ring is called [[finitely generated module|finitely generated]] if every element is a linear combination of a fixed finite number of elements. If the module happens to be an algebra this is much stronger than saying it is finitely generated as an algebra.

definition: The Fitting ideal In(M) of a module M generated by g elements is the ideal generated by the determinants of the minors of size g–n of the matrix of relations defining the module.
definition_raw: The [[Fitting ideal]] ''I''<sub>''n''</sub>(''M'') of a module ''M'' generated by ''g'' elements is the ideal generated by the determinants of the minors of size ''g''–''n'' of the matrix of relations defining the module.

definition: A flat module is a module such that tensoring with it preserves exactness.
definition_raw: A [[flat module]] is a module such that tensoring with it preserves exactness.

definition: A homomorphism f:A→B of rings is called formally smooth, formally unramified, or formally etale if for every A-algebra R with a nilpotent ideal I, the natural map from HomA(R/I, B) to HomA(R, B) is surjective, injective, or bijective. The algebra B is then called a formally smooth, formally unramified, or formally etale A-algebra.
definition_raw: A homomorphism ''f'':''A''→''B'' of rings is called [[formally smooth]], [[formally unramified]], or [[formally etale]] if for every ''A''-algebra ''R'' with a nilpotent ideal ''I'', the natural map from Hom<sub>''A''</sub>(''R''/''I'', ''B'') to Hom<sub>''A''</sub>(''R'', ''B'') is surjective, injective, or bijective. The algebra ''B'' is then called a formally smooth, formally unramified, or formally etale ''A''-algebra.

definition: If K is the ring of fractions of an integral domain R, then a fractional ideal of R is a submodule of the R-module K contained in kR for some k in K.
definition_raw: If ''K'' is the ring of fractions of an integral domain ''R'', then a [[fractional ideal]] of ''R'' is a submodule of the ''R''-module ''K'' contained in ''kR'' for some ''k'' in ''K''.

definition: An alternative name for fractional ideals
definition_raw: An alternative name for [[fractional ideal]]s

definition: An alternative name for a Grothendieck ring.
definition_raw: An alternative name for a [[Grothendieck ring]].

definition: The Gaussian ring is the ring of Gaussian integers m+ni.
definition_raw: The [[Gaussian ring]] is the ring of [[Gaussian integer]]s ''m''+''ni''.

definition: Abbreviation for greatest common divisor
definition_raw: Abbreviation for [[greatest common divisor]]

definition: The word "geometrically" usually refers to properties that continue to hold after taking finite field extensions. For example, a ring R over a field k is called geometrically normal, geometrically regular, or geometrically reduced if R⊗kK is normal, regular, or reduced for every finite extension field K of k.
definition_raw: The word "geometrically" usually refers to properties that continue to hold after taking finite field extensions. For example, a ring ''R'' over a field ''k'' is called geometrically normal, [[geometrically regular]], or geometrically reduced if ''R''⊗<sub>''k''</sub>''K'' is normal, regular, or reduced for every finite extension field ''K'' of ''k''.

definition: An extension R⊆S of commutative rings is said to have the going down property if whenever p1⊆p2 is a chain of prime ideals in R and q2 is a prime ideal of S with q2∩R=p2, there is a prime ideal q1 of S with q1⊆q2 and q1∩R=p1.
definition_raw: An extension ''R''⊆''S'' of commutative rings is said to have the [[going down property]] if whenever ''p''<sub>1</sub>⊆''p''<sub>2</sub> is a chain of prime ideals in ''R'' and ''q''<sub>2</sub> is a prime ideal of ''S'' with ''q''<sub>2</sub>∩''R''=''p''<sub>2</sub>, there is a prime ideal ''q''<sub>1</sub> of ''S'' with ''q''<sub>1</sub>⊆''q''<sub>2</sub> and ''q''<sub>1</sub>∩''R''=''p''<sub>1</sub>.

definition: An extension R⊆S of commutative rings is said to have the going up property if whenever p1⊆p2 is a chain of prime ideals in R and q1 is a prime ideal of S with q1∩R=p1, there is a prime ideal q2 of S with q1⊆q2 and q2∩R=p2.
definition_raw: An extension ''R''⊆''S'' of commutative rings is said to have the [[going up property]] if whenever ''p''<sub>1</sub>⊆''p''<sub>2</sub> is a chain of prime ideals in ''R'' and ''q''<sub>1</sub> is a prime ideal of ''S'' with ''q''<sub>1</sub>∩''R''=''p''<sub>1</sub>, there is a prime ideal ''q''<sub>2</sub> of ''S'' with ''q''<sub>1</sub>⊆''q''<sub>2</sub> and ''q''<sub>2</sub>∩''R''=''p''<sub>2</sub>.

definition: A Gröbner basis is a set of generators for an ideal of a polynomial ring satisfying certain conditions.

definition: A module $M$ is idealwise separated for an ideal I if for every ideal, $\mathfrak{a}$, $\bigcap_{n \ge 1} I^n(\mathfrak{a} \otimes M) = 0$ (for example, this is the case when A is a Noetherian local ring, I its maximal ideal and M finitely generated).
definition_raw: A module <math>M</math> is idealwise separated for an ideal ''I'' if for every ideal, <math>\mathfrak{a}</math>, <math>\bigcap_{n \ge 1} I^n(\mathfrak{a} \otimes M) = 0</math>  (for example, this is the case when ''A'' is a Noetherian [[local ring]], ''I'' its [[maximal ideal]] and ''M'' finitely generated).<ref>{{Cite book |last=Matsumura |first=Hideyuki |title=Commutative algebra |date=1981 |publisher=Benjamin/Cummings |isbn=978-0-8053-7026-3 |edition=2. ed., 2. print |series=Mathematics lectu

definition: The extension A⊆B is said to satisfy the incomparability property if whenever Q and Q' are distinct primes of B lying over prime P in A, then Q⊈Q'<nowiki /> and Q'<nowiki />⊈Q.

definition: A module is called indecomposable if it is not the direct sum of two proper submodules.

definition: An inertia group is a group of automorphisms of a ring whose elements fix a given prime ideal and act trivially on the corresponding residue class ring.

definition: In a graded ring, the initial ideal of an ideal I is the set of all homogeneous components of minimal degree of the elements in I (this is an ideal of the multiplicative monoid of the homogeneous elements.)

definition: An injective module is one with the property that maps from submodules to it can be extended to larger modules.
definition_raw: An [[injective module]] is one with the property that maps from submodules to it can be extended to larger modules.

definition: The two different meanings of integral (no zero divisors, or every element being a root of a monic polynomial) are sometimes confused.

definition: An invertible fractional ideal is a fractional ideal that has an inverse in the monoid of fractional ideals under multiplication.

definition: An element of a ring is called irreducible if it cannot be written as a product of two non-units.

definition: The irrelevant ideal of a graded algebra is generated by all elements of positive degree.
definition_raw: The [[irrelevant ideal]] of a graded algebra is generated by all elements of positive degree.

definition: An isolated prime of a module is a minimal associated prime.
definition_raw: An [[isolated prime]] of a module is a minimal associated prime.

definition: A J-0 ring is a ring such that the set of regular points of the spectrum contains a non-empty open subset.

definition: A J-1 ring is a ring such that the set of regular points of the spectrum is an open subset.

definition: The Jacobian matrix is a matrix whose entries are the partial derivatives of some polynomials.

definition: A Japanese ring (also called N-2 ring) is an integral domain R such that for every finite extension L of its quotient field K, the integral closure of R in L is a finitely generated R module.

A [[Japanese ring]] (also called N-2 ring) is
an [[integral domain]] ''R'' such that for every
[[field extension#Definitions|finite extension]] ''L'' of its quotient field ''K'', the integral closure of ''R'' in ''L'' is a finitely generated  ''R'' module.

definition: The module of Kähler differentials of a ring is the universal module with a derivation from the ring to it.
definition_raw: The module of [[Kähler differential]]s of a ring is the universal module with a derivation from the ring to it.

definition: The Kleinian integers are the integers of the imaginary quadratic field of discriminant −7.
definition_raw: The [[Kleinian integers]] are the integers of the imaginary quadratic field of discriminant −7.

definition: The Koszul complex is a free resolution constructed from a regular sequence.

definition: A Krull ring (or Krull domain) is a ring with a well behaved theory of prime factorization.

definition: A Laskerian ring is a ring in which any ideal has a primary decomposition.

definition: The length of a module is the length of any composition series.

definition: Two subfields of a field extension K over a field k are called linearly disjoint if the natural map from their tensor product over k to the subfield of K they generate is an isomorphism.
definition_raw: Two subfields of a field extension ''K'' over a field ''k'' are called [[linearly disjoint]] if the natural map from their tensor product over ''k'' to the subfield of ''K'' they generate is an isomorphism.

definition: A local ring is a ring with just one maximal ideal. In older books it is sometimes also assumed to be Noetherian.

definition: An extension of rings has the lying over property if the corresponding map between their prime spectra is surjective.

definition: Miracle flatness is another name for Hironaka's criterion, which says that a local ring that is finite over a regular local ring is Cohen-Macaulay if and only if it is a flat module.
definition_raw: Miracle flatness is another name for [[Hironaka's criterion]], which says that a local ring that is finite  over a regular local ring is [[Cohen-Macaulay]] if and only if it is a flat module.

definition: A Nagata ring is a Noetherian universally Japanese ring. These are also called pseudo-geometric rings.
definition_raw: A [[Nagata ring]] is a Noetherian universally Japanese ring. These are also called pseudo-geometric rings.

definition: Nakayama's lemma states that if a finitely generated module M is equal to IM where I is the Jacobson radical, then M is zero.

definition: A prime ideal is a proper ideal whose complement is closed under multiplication.

definition: A primary ideal is a proper ideal p of a ring R such that if rm is in p then either m is in p or some power of r is in p. More generally a primary submodule of a module M is a submodule N of M such that if rm is in N then either m is in N or some power of r annihilates N.
definition_raw: A '''[[primary ideal]]''' is a proper ideal ''p'' of a ring ''R'' such that if ''rm'' is in ''p'' then either ''m'' is in ''p'' or some power of ''r'' is in ''p''. More generally a primary submodule of a module ''M'' is a submodule ''N'' of ''M'' such that if ''rm'' is in ''N'' then either ''m'' is in ''N'' or some power of ''r'' annihilates ''N''.

definition: A principal ideal is an ideal generated by one element.
definition_raw: A [[principal ideal]] is an ideal generated by one element.

definition: A projective module is a module such that every epimorphism to it splits.
definition_raw: A [[projective module]] is a module such that every epimorphism to it splits.

definition: A Prüfer domain is a semiherediary integral domain.
definition_raw: A [[Prüfer domain]] is a semiherediary integral domain.

definition: A finitely generated module M is called pseudo-zero if <math>M_{\mathfrak{p
definition_raw: A finitely generated module ''M'' is called '''pseudo-zero''' if <math>M_{\mathfrak{p

definition: A pure submodule M of a module N is a submodule such that M⊗A is a submodule of N⊗A for all modules A.
definition_raw: A [[pure submodule]] ''M'' of a module ''N'' is a submodule such that ''M''⊗''A'' is a submodule of ''N''⊗''A'' for all modules ''A''.

definition: An element x is purely inseparable over a field if either the field has characteristic zero and x is in the field or the field has characteristic p and $x^{p^r}$ is in the field for some r.
definition_raw: An element ''x'' is [[purely inseparable]] over a field if either the field has characteristic zero and ''x'' is in the field or the field has characteristic ''p'' and <math>x^{p^r}</math> is in the field for some ''r''.

definition: A quasi-excellent ring is a Grothendieck ring such that for every finitely generated algebra the singular points of the spectrum form a closed subset.
definition_raw: A [[quasi-excellent ring]] is a Grothendieck ring such that for every finitely generated algebra the singular points of the spectrum form a closed subset.

definition: A quotient of a ring by an ideal, or of a module by a submodule.
definition_raw: A quotient of a ring by an ideal, or of a module by a submodule.

definition: The condition Rn on a ring (for a non-negative integer n), "regular in codimension n", says that localization at any prime ideal of height at most n is regular. (cf. Serre's criterion on normality)
definition_raw: The condition ''R''<sub>''n''</sub> on a ring (for a non-negative integer ''n''), "regular in codimension ''n''", says that localization at any prime ideal of height at most ''n'' is regular. (cf. [[Serre's criterion on normality]])

definition: The Jacobson radical of a ring.
definition_raw: The [[Jacobson radical]] of a ring.

definition: A ramification group is a group of automorphisms of a ring R fixing some given prime ideal p and acting trivially on R/pn for some integer n>1. (When n=1 it is called the inertia group.)
definition_raw: A [[ramification group]] is a group of automorphisms of a ring ''R'' fixing some given prime ideal ''p'' and acting trivially on ''R''/''p''<sup>''n''</sup> for some integer ''n''>1. (When ''n''=1 it is called the inertia group.)

definition: Another older name for the height of a prime ideal.
definition_raw: Another older name for the height of a prime ideal.

definition: A reduced ring is one with no non-zero nilpotent elements.
definition_raw: A [[reduced ring]] is one with no non-zero nilpotent elements.

definition: See irreducible.
definition_raw: See [[#irreducible|irreducible]].

definition: A reduction ideal of an ideal I with respect to a module M is an ideal J with JInM=In+1M for some positive integer n.
definition_raw: A reduction ideal of an ideal ''I'' with respect to a module ''M'' is an ideal ''J'' with ''JI''<sup>''n''</sup>''M''=''I''<sup>''n''+1</sup>''M'' for some positive integer ''n''.

definition: Named after David Rees
definition_raw: Named after [[David Rees (mathematician)|David Rees]]

definition: A module M is reflexive if the canonical map $M \to M^{**}, m \mapsto \langle \cdot, m \rangle$ is an isomorphism.
definition_raw: A module ''M'' is [[reflexive module|reflexive]] if the canonical map <math>M \to M^{**}, m \mapsto \langle \cdot, m \rangle</math> is an isomorphism.

definition: A regular local ring is a Noetherian local ring whose dimension is equal to the dimension of its tangent space.
definition_raw: A [[regular local ring]] is a Noetherian local ring whose dimension is equal to the dimension of its tangent space.

definition: Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity is an invariant of a graded module over a graded ring related to the vanishing of various cohomology groups.
definition_raw: [[Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity]] is an invariant of a graded module over a graded ring related to the vanishing of various cohomology groups.

definition: The quotient of a ring, especially a local ring, by a maximal ideal.
definition_raw: The quotient of a ring, especially a local ring, by a maximal ideal.

definition: A resolution of a module is a chain complex whose only non-zero homology group is the module.
definition_raw: A [[resolution of a module]] is a chain complex whose only non-zero homology group is the module.

definition: The condition Sn on a ring (for a non-negative integer n) says that the depth of the localization at any prime ideal is the height of the prime ideal whenever the depth is less than n. (cf. Serre's criterion on normality)
definition_raw: The condition ''S''<sub>''n''</sub> on a ring (for a non-negative integer ''n'') says that the depth of the localization at any prime ideal is the height of the prime ideal whenever the depth is less than ''n''. (cf. [[Serre's criterion on normality]])

definition: A subset X of a ring or module is called saturated with respect to a multiplicative subset S if xs in X and s in S implies that x is in X.
definition_raw: A subset ''X'' of a ring or module is called saturated with respect to a multiplicative subset ''S'' if ''xs'' in ''X'' and ''s'' in ''S'' implies that ''x'' is in ''X''.

definition: The saturation of a subset of a ring or module is the smallest saturated subset containing it.
definition_raw: The saturation of a subset of a ring or module is the smallest saturated subset containing it.

definition: A semilocal ring is a ring with only a finite number of maximal ideals.
definition_raw: A [[semilocal ring]] is a ring with only a finite number of maximal ideals.

definition: A semilocal ring is a ring with only a finite number of maximal ideals.
definition_raw: A [[semilocal ring]] is a ring with only a finite number of maximal ideals.

definition: A seminormal ring is a commutative reduced ring in which, whenever x, y satisfy $x^3 = y^2$, there is s with $s^2 = x$ and $s^3 = y$.
definition_raw: A [[seminormal ring]] is a commutative [[reduced ring]] in which, whenever ''x'', ''y'' satisfy <math>x^3 = y^2</math>, there is ''s'' with <math>s^2 = x</math> and <math>s^3 = y</math>.

definition: An algebra over a field is called separable if its extension by any finite purely inseparable extension is reduced.
definition_raw: An algebra over a field is called separable if its extension by any finite purely inseparable extension is reduced.

definition: An alternative term for Hausdorff, usually applied to a topology on a ring or module.
definition_raw: An alternative term for [[Hausdorff topology|Hausdorff]], usually applied to a topology on a ring or module.

definition: A simple field is an archaic term for an algebraic number field whose ring of integers is a unique factorization domain.
definition_raw: A '''simple field''' is an archaic term for an algebraic number field whose ring of integers is a unique factorization domain.

definition: Not regular
definition_raw: Not regular

definition: A smooth morphism of rings is a homomorphism that is formally smooth and finitely presented. These are analogous to submersions in differential topology. An algebra over a ring is called smooth if the corresponding morphism is smooth.
definition_raw:
A [[smooth morphism]] of rings is a homomorphism that is formally smooth and finitely presented.
These are analogous to submersions in differential topology. An algebra over a ring is called smooth if the corresponding morphism is smooth.

definition: The socle of a module is the sum of its simple submodules.
definition_raw: The [[socle of a module]] is the sum of its simple submodules.

definition: The prime spectrum of a ring, often just called the spectrum, is a locally ringed space whose underlying topological space is the set of prime ideals with the Zariski topology.
definition_raw: The [[prime spectrum]] of a ring, often just called the spectrum,  is a locally ringed space whose underlying topological space is the set of prime ideals with the Zariski topology.

definition: A decreasing filtration of a module is called stable (with respect to an ideal I) if Mn+1=IMn for all sufficiently large n.
definition_raw: A decreasing filtration of a module is called stable (with respect to an ideal ''I'') if ''M''<sub>''n''+1</sub>=''IM''<sub>''n''</sub> for all sufficiently large ''n''.

definition: A module M over a ring R is called stably free if M⊕Rn is free for some natural number n.
definition_raw: A module ''M'' over a ring ''R'' is called [[stably free]] if ''M''⊕''R''<sup>''n''</sup> is free for some natural number ''n''.

definition: Named after Richard P. Stanley
definition_raw: Named after [[Richard P. Stanley]]

definition: A ring is called strictly local if it is a local Henselian ring whose residue field is separably closed.
definition_raw: A ring is called strictly local if it is a local Henselian ring whose residue field is separably closed.

definition: A submodule M of N is called superfluous if M+X=N implies X=N (for submodules X).
definition_raw: A submodule ''M'' of ''N'' is called [[Superfluous submodule|superfluous]] if ''M''+''X''=''N'' implies ''X''=''N'' (for submodules ''X'').

definition: The superheight of an ideal is the supremum of the nonzero codimensions of the proper extensions of the ideal under ring homomorphisms.
definition_raw: The superheight of an ideal is the supremum of the nonzero codimensions of the proper extensions of the ideal under ring homomorphisms.

definition: The support of a module M is the set of prime ideals p such that the localization of M at p is non-zero.
definition_raw: The [[support of a module]] ''M'' is the set of prime ideals ''p'' such that the localization of ''M'' at ''p'' is non-zero.

definition: The symbolic power p(n) of a prime ideal p is the set of elements x such that xy is in pn for some y not in p. It is the smallest p-primary ideal containing pn.
definition_raw: The [[Primary ideal|symbolic power]] ''p''<sup>(''n'')</sup> of a prime ideal ''p'' is the set of elements ''x'' such that ''xy'' is in ''p''<sup>''n''</sup> for some ''y'' not in ''p''. It is the smallest ''p''-primary ideal containing ''p''<sup>''n''</sup>.

definition: A set of dim R (if finite) elements of a local ring R with maximal ideal m that generates an m-primary ideal. It is a regular system of parameters if it actually generates m.
definition_raw: A set of dim ''R'' (if finite) elements of a local ring ''R'' with maximal ideal ''m'' that generates an ''m''-primary ideal. It is a '''regular system of parameters''' if it actually generates ''m''.

definition: An element of the kernel of one of the maps in a free resolution of a module.
definition_raw: An element of the kernel of one of the maps in a free resolution of a module.

definition: The Zariski tangent space of a local ring is the dual of its cotangent space.
definition_raw: The [[Zariski tangent space]] of a local ring is the dual of its cotangent space.

definition: The tight closure I* of an ideal I of a ring with positive characteristic p>0 consists of the elements z such that there is some c not in any minimal prime ideal such that czq is in I[q] for all sufficiently large powers q of p, where I[q] is the ideal generated by all qth powers of elements of I.
definition_raw: The [[tight closure]] ''I''* of an ideal ''I'' of a ring with  positive characteristic ''p''>0 consists of the elements ''z'' such that there is some ''c'' not in any minimal prime ideal such that ''cz''<sup>''q''</sup> is in ''I''<sup>[''q'']</sup> for all sufficiently large powers ''q'' of ''p'', where ''I''<sup>[''q'']</sup> is the ideal generated by all ''q''th powers of elements of ''I''.

definition: The Torsion functors, the derived functors of the tensor product.
definition_raw: The [[Torsion functor]]s, the derived functors of the tensor product.

definition: A torsion element of a module over a ring is an element annihilated by some regular element of the ring.
definition_raw: A [[torsion element]] of a module over a ring is an element annihilated by some regular element of the ring.

definition: The total ring of fractions or total quotient ring of a ring is formed by forcing all non zero divisors to have inverses.
definition_raw: The [[total ring of fractions]] or [[total quotient ring]] of a ring is formed by forcing all non zero divisors to have inverses.

definition: A trivial ring is a ring with only one element.
definition_raw: A trivial ring is a ring with only one element.

definition: The type of a finitely generated module M of depth d over a Noetherian local ring R with residue field k is the dimension (over k) of Ext(k,M).
definition_raw: The type of a finitely generated module ''M'' of depth ''d'' over a Noetherian local ring ''R'' with residue field ''k'' is the dimension (over ''k'') of Ext{{su|p=''d''|b=''R''}}(''k'',''M'').

definition: Abbreviation for unique factorization domain.
definition_raw: Abbreviation for [[unique factorization domain]].

definition: A reduced local ring is called unibranch if it is integral and its integral closure is a local ring. A local ring is called unibranch if the corresponding reduced local ring is unibranch.
definition_raw: A reduced local ring is called [[Unibranch local ring|unibranch]] if it is integral and its integral closure is a local ring. A local ring is called unibranch if the corresponding reduced local ring is unibranch.

definition: A sequence of elements $v_1, \dots, v_n$ in a ring that generate the unit ideal.
definition_raw: A sequence of elements <math>v_1, \dots, v_n</math> in a ring that generate the unit ideal.

definition: Also called a factorial domain. A unique factorization domain is an integral domain such that every element can be written as a product of primes in a way that is unique up to order and multiplication by units.
definition_raw: Also called a factorial domain. A [[unique factorization domain]] is an integral domain such that every element can be written as a product of primes in a way that is unique up to order and multiplication by units.

definition: A property is said to hold universally if it holds for various base changes. For example a ring is universally catenary if all finitely generated algebras over it are catenary.
definition_raw: A property is said to hold universally if it holds for various base changes. For example a ring is [[universally catenary]] if all finitely generated algebras over it are catenary.

definition: A universal field is an algebraically closed field with the uncountable transcendence degree over its prime field.
definition_raw: A universal field is an algebraically closed field with the uncountable transcendence degree over its prime field.

definition: An ideal I of a ring R is called unmixed if all associated primes of R/I have the same height.
definition_raw: An ideal ''I'' of a ring ''R'' is called unmixed if all associated primes of ''R''/''I'' have the same height.

definition: An unramified morphism of rings is a homomorphism that is formally unramified and finitely presented. These are analogous to immersions in differential topology. An algebra over a ring is called unramified if the corresponding morphism is unramified.
definition_raw:
An [[unramified morphism]] of rings is a homomorphism that is formally unramified and finitely presented.
These are analogous to immersions in differential topology. An algebra over a ring is called unramified if the corresponding morphism is unramified.

definition: A valuation is a homomorphism from the non-zero elements of a field to a totally ordered abelian group, with properties similar to the p-adic valuation of the rational numbers.
definition_raw: A [[valuation (algebra)|valuation]] is a homomorphism from the non-zero elements of a field to a totally ordered abelian group, with properties similar to the ''p''-adic valuation of the rational numbers.

definition: Weak dimension is an alternative name for flat dimension of a module.
definition_raw: Weak dimension is an alternative name for flat dimension of a module.

definition: A Weierstrass ring is local ring that is Henselian, pseudo-geometric, and such that any quotient ring by a prime ideal is a finite extension of a regular local ring.
definition_raw: A [[Weierstrass ring]] is local ring that is Henselian, pseudo-geometric, and such that any quotient ring by a prime ideal is a finite extension of a regular local ring.

definition: Named after Oscar Zariski
definition_raw: Named after [[Oscar Zariski]]

definition: A zero divisor in a ring is an element whose product with some nonzero element is 0.
definition_raw: A [[zero divisor]] in a ring is an element whose product with some nonzero element is 0.

definition: – A basic element $x$ with respect to an element $y$ is an element of a cochain complex $(C^*, d)$ (e.g., complex of differential forms on a manifold) that is closed: $dx = 0$ and the contraction of $x$ by $y$ is zero.
source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology

definition: – Given two differentiable manifolds $M$ and $N$, a bijective map $f$ from $M$ to $N$ is called a diffeomorphism – if both $f:M\to N$ and its inverse $f^{-1}:N\to M$ are smooth functions.
source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology
definition_raw: – Given two [[Manifold#Differentiable manifolds|differentiable manifolds]] ''<math>M</math>'' and ''<math>N</math>'', a [[bijective map]] <math>f</math> from ''<math>M</math>'' to ''<math>N</math>'' is called a '''diffeomorphism''' – if both <math>f:M\to N</math> and its inverse <math>f^{-1}:N\to M</math> are [[smooth function]]s.

source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology

definition: – Given a manifold $M$ with boundary, doubling is taking two copies of $M$ and identifying their boundaries. As the result we get a manifold without boundary.
source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology
definition_raw: – Given a manifold ''<math>M</math>'' with boundary, doubling is taking two copies of ''<math>M</math>'' and identifying their boundaries. As the result we get a manifold without boundary.

definition: – In a fiber bundle, $\pi:E \to B$ the preimage $\pi^{-1}(x)$ of a point $x$ in the base $B$ is called the fiber over $x$, often denoted $E_x$.
source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology

definition: – A frame at a point of a differentiable manifold M is a basis of the tangent space at the point.

definition: – A topological manifold is a locally Euclidean Hausdorff space (usually also required to be second-countable). For a given regularity (e.g. piecewise-linear, $C^k$ or $C^\infty$ differentiable, real or complex analytic, Lipschitz, Hölder, quasi-conformal...), a manifold of that regularity is a topological manifold whose charts transitions have the prescribed regularity.

definition: – A Whitney sum is an analog of the direct product for vector bundles. Given two vector bundles $\alpha$ and $\beta$ over the same base $B$ their cartesian product is a vector bundle over $B\times B$. The diagonal map $B\to B\times B$ induces a vector bundle over $B$ called the Whitney sum of these vector bundles and denoted by $\alpha \oplus \beta$.
source_glossary: Glossary of differential geometry and topology
definition_raw: – A Whitney sum is an analog of the [[direct product]] for vector bundles. Given two vector bundles ''<math>\alpha</math>'' and ''<math>\beta</math>'' over the same base ''<math>B</math>'' their [[cartesian product]] is a vector bundle over <math>B\times B</math>. The diagonal map <math>B\to B\times B</math> induces a vector bundle over ''<math>B</math>'' called the Whitney sum of these vector bundles and denoted by ''<math>\alpha \oplus \beta</math>''.
